title: Eriosyce megliolii
taxonomy: Eriosyce megliolii is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. It is part of the genus Eriosyce, which is a group of South American cacti. The genus Eriosyce was previously part of the genus Neowerkhia, but it was reclassified in 1963. The classification of Eriosyce megliolii places it within the subfamily Cactoideae, which contains a diverse range of cacti species. Further classification includes its family and order, which are Cactaceae and Caryophyllales, respectively.

intro: Eriosyce megliolii is a species of cactus, specifically a type of small, globular cactus native to the Atacama Desert in Chile. Traditionally, Eriosyce megliolii has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Eriosyce megliolii include teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat respiratory issues and promote wound healing.
